transcript: Do you have a... personal computer at work?
Do you have access to the internet at work?

'Kay, thinking about potential Y2K computer problems, how concerned are you that these problems might limit your ability to purchase food and other grocery items: very concerned, concerned, not too concerned, not concerned at all, or haven't heard of the problem?
[clears throat] Well, I've, uh... created a very large microwave. It's about two hundred square...
Um... and, uh... New Year's Eve, I intend to enter that... large chamber...
'Kay. So would you be very concerned, concerned, not too concerned, or not concerned at all?
Okay. Are you planning on stocking up on any grocery items because of your potential Y2K... K problems: Definitely will, probably will, probably will not, or definitely--
Probably will maybe will not could... could not.
Well... I can only write one.
I mean, do you think I should not, or...?
[laughing] That's up to you. I'm just saying, are you planning... on... stocking up on any grocery items--
I'll be in a hot air balloon at that time.
[laughs] Okay... um... now just a few questions to organize the information you've given me. Which of the following categories includes your age: eighteen to thirty-four--
How many people reside in your household?
Eighteen people live in your household?
Yeah, you just caught me at... a good time.

Oh. Which of the following categories includes your annual household income: less than twenty-five thousand, twenty-five to fifty thousand, fifty to seventy-five thousand, seventy-five to one hundred thousand, over one hundred thousand?
Okay, let me just verify your phone number then so nobody calls you back, and that's... so no more surveys. They call you at area code-- '[tape speed slows]' --303... '[scramble]'
